entry.title: Embedded Launch Activities: Expanding the Customer Onboarding Journey
entry.content:
Embedded Launch Activities: Expanding the Customer Onboarding Journey

From March 2 onwards, SAP expands Embedded Launch Activities with an enhanced onboarding experience designed to accelerate time to value from contract start and enable implementation teams to engage hands-on with the system at the earliest possible stage.

This expanded framework supports all customers subscribing to any SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition bundle and provides a structured onboarding journey through 1:1 sessions, quarterly 1:m live sessions, and self-service resources. As SAP solutions continue to evolve, so does the Embedded Launch Activities framework — continuously expanding in scope, depth, and relevance.

The Customer Onboarding Journey

1:1 Onboarding Sessions

We engage directly with every customer, always involving implementation partners who bring deep expertise in SAP solutions, industry context, and customer business processes.

These sessions establish a shared understanding across all stakeholders, covering:

  • Project scope and key milestones

  • System architecture and provisioning foundations

  • Commercial entitlements included in the subscription

  • An introduction to artificial intelligence capabilities embedded in the product

  • An overview of SAP Support and engagement models

This early alignment lays the groundwork for a confident and well-structured implementation.

1:1 Entitlement Deep-Dive Sessions

As part of the current commercial bundles, customers have access to additional solutions across treasury, expense reporting, sustainability, and extensibility through SAP Build.

To help implementation teams understand both the value and the implementation path of these solutions, dedicated specialist 1:1 sessions are available, including:

  • SAP Build

  • SAP Taulia

  • SAP Concur

  • SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity

  • Sustainability solutions within Supply Chain Premium

These sessions ensure that entitlements translate into practical adoption and measurable business outcomes.

Quality Gates with SAP Cloud ALM

A core element of the expanded framework is guided enablement in setting up quality gates within SAP Cloud ALM.

Through shared-screen sessions, customers are guided to:

  • Define critical checkpoints across the project lifecycle

  • Assess readiness and quality of deliverables

  • Validate compliance with requirements

  • Confirm that key tasks and documentation meet expected standards

Quality gates provide structured decision points that support informed go/no-go decisions and reduce delivery risk.

Alignment Through the Onboarding Plan

The onboarding journey concludes with a dedicated 1:1 session focused on alignment around the Onboarding Plan, ensuring clarity on next steps and ongoing support.

Customers are also encouraged to participate in 1:m live sessions (with recordings available), covering topics such as:

  • Project methodology and implementation best practices

  • End-to-end system architecture

  • SAP product roadmaps

  • Influence SAP and SAP Communities

  • Upgrade cycles and continuous innovation

  • Tools that support sustained value from ERP, including ongoing AI innovations

Guided Enablement and Continuous Learning

As part of the Embedded Launch Activities framework, customers also gain access to Guided Enablement, a structured learning program for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition onboarding.

This program helps implementation teams track progress and build capability throughout the project lifecycle. The learning content is continuously updated to reflect product evolution and emerging best practices.

The Digital Baseline

The Onboarding Resource Center serves as the digital baseline for the onboarding journey. Customers are introduced through a welcome email and subsequently contacted by an Onboarding Advisor to initiate the journey and guide them through each phase.

Embedded Launch Activities provide a consistent, scalable, and evolving onboarding experience — designed to support customers from day one and grow alongside their SAP landscape.

entry.title: How Do I Activate a Situation Type in the Extended Framework?
entry.content:
Situation Handling notifies users about issues requiring their attention. You may choose SAP delivered situation templates or design your own use cases. For the extended framework for Situation Handling, we often discussed how you can create custom situation templates. The framework comes also with SAP delivered use cases that you can easily activate.

This blog post explains how you can activate situation types from SAP delivered situation templates with the extended framework for Situation Handling. It has been available since SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2202 and SAP S/4HANA 2021 FPS02.

 

SAP Delivered Use Cases

Looking for Situation Handling use cases? Visit SAP Help and navigate to Situation Handling, then choose Use Cases. You can narrow your results by filtering for Situation Template Type (either extended object-based or message-based), area, and topic. To learn more about a specific use case, simply check the link in the Situation Template column for a detailed description.

Use Case

For illustration purposes we use the situation template Expiration of Purchase Contracts (PROC_ABOUT_TO_EXPIRE_CTR). Purchasers get a heads-up for purchase contracts that are about to expire.

 

Activation Overview for 2SL and 3SL

Before discussing the activation steps in detail, let me point out the differences between system landscapes and their specific transport paths.

In the extended framework for Situation Handling, you create a situation type from a situation template. Situation templates are only visible in the quality system of a 2-system landscape (2SL) and the development system of a  3-system-landscape (3SL). The situation type needs to be transported to the production system. The transport paths differ between 2SL and 3SL.

Transport the Situation Type in 2SL

In a 2-system-landscape, you transport the situation type from the quality system to the production system.

  • In the quality system you
    • Create a situation type from a situation template
    • Test if the configuration alerts the right users about the situation
    • Export the well-defined situation type
  • In the production system you
    • Import the situation type
    • Since the data usually differs between quality and production system you need to enter the appropriate values for the condition filters
    • Enable the situation type

Transports in 2-System-Landscape

Transport the Situation Type in 3SL

In a 3-system-landscape, transports are required from the development system to both the test and production system. In a 3SL environment you may also have different tenants for development, customizing, and parallel customizing that need to be considered as well.

  • On the customizing tenant in the development system you
    • Create a situation type from a situation template
    • Export the situation type
  • On the test tenant in the test system you
    • Import the situation type
    • Since the data usually differs between systems you need to enter the appropriate values for the condition filters
    • Test if the configuration alerts the right users about the situation
    • Forward the well-defined situation type
  • On the production tenant in the production system you
    • Import the forwarded situation type
    • Enter the appropriate values for the condition filters
    • Enable the situation type

Transports in 3-System-Landscape

Detailed Activation of a Situation Type in 3SL

Now let’s check the detailed steps for a sample use case in a 3-system-landscape as it is more comprehensive. In 2SL the export / import mechanism resembles the one from development system to test system in 3SL.

Depending on your system setting, the transports may also be processed automatically. For optimal clarity, the blog post discusses the transport steps through manual triggers.

Development System

Create a Situation Type from a Situation Template

Log-in as business process configuration expert on the customizing tenant in the development system. Open the Manage Situation Types – Extended app where you see a list of SAP delivered templates. Use the search option to find the template for our use case Expiration of Purchase Contracts (PROC_ABOUT_TO_EXPIRE_CTR). Select the template and an object page opens. In the header, select Create Situation Type.

Create situation type from situation template

A new page opens with the situation type that contains all information from the situation template. You just need to enter an ID, Name and Description. The type is configured and ready to use. Depending on your requirements you could adapt the settings, for example, by adding filter values for the conditions, changing texts or rescheduling the batch job.

I recommend monitoring the instances to see how situations are handled.

Monitor situations

Let’s check the relevant settings and understand how the configuration works.

There are two Situation Triggers that detect situations and cover different usage scenarios. While the batch-based trigger checks for situations in the background, the event-based trigger reacts on every active change of the contract.

Situation triggers

The situation triggers cover the following cases:

  • The Event-Based Trigger checks the conditions whenever you create or update a purchase contract.
    • Condition 1 checks if the validity period of a purchase contract expires within the next 30 days and triggers a situation. The information displayed to the user is defined in Situation Display number 1.
    • Condition 2 is only executed if condition 1 did not match. That is, the validity period is not within the next 30 days, for instance when the contract got extended, and the situation is closed. As nobody will be informed about the closure, the texts are empty (Situation Display number 2).

Event-based trigger

  • In addition to the events, the Batch-Based Trigger regularly checks the validity end date according to the batch schedule.
    • Condition 1 checks if the validity period of a purchase contract expires within the next 30 days and triggers a situation.

Batch-based trigger

The configuration for the Batch Job Scheduling specifies that the contract dates are verified every 3 hours throughout the day, beginning at 6:00 a.m.

Batch job scheduling

Export the Situation Type

Log-in as administrator on the customizing tenant in the development system. Open the Export Software Collection app.

  • On top of the table choose Create Software Collection and a new page opens
  • Choose Add Items and select the situation type you created
  • On the header select Check and wait until the check is processed successfully
  • Then choose Export and wait until the export is finished successfully

Test System

Import the Situation Type

Log-in as administrator on the test tenant in the test system. Open the Import Collection app.

  • Choose your software collection and a new page opens
  • On the header select Manual Import and wait until the check is processed successfully
  • Then choose Import on the bottom and wait until the import is finished successfully

Test the Situation Type

As business process specialist you can test the situation type on the corresponding tenant.

Make sure that the situation type is enabled.

Activate the situation type

Then maintain the conditions. The filter values are not transported since they may differ in the various systems and tenants.

  • Select the Event-Based Trigger and a new page opens
    • Condition 1 select Edit and enter the filter values
      • Ext. Reference ID: enter Empty, using the value help
      • Validity Period End: enter Next30Days, using the value help
    • Condition 2 select Edit and enter the filter values
      • Ext. Reference ID: enter Empty, using the value help
    • Go back to the main page

Event-based trigger

  • Select the Batch-Based Trigger and a new page opens
    • Condition 1 select Edit and enter the filter values
      • Ext. Reference ID: enter Empty, using the value help
      • Validity Period End: enter Next30Days, using the value help
    • Go back to the main page

Batch-based trigger

With the situation triggers, you defined when a situation is detected. Now, you need to define who should be informed about situations. Maintain the team(s) in the Manage Teams and Responsibilities app and ensure that at least one team is enabled. The filter settings work the same way as with the standard framework for Situation Handling.

Situation recipients

Now you can test the situation type in your business area with the corresponding application data. You can check if there are purchase contracts that match the conditions by choosing Trigger Batch and Manage Instances.

Test the type with trigger batch

If no situations were detected you can log-in as purchaser and create or manipulate a purchasing contract. Add an end date within the next 30 days and the save event will trigger a situation.

When you log-in as a team member of the recipients team you can also check, if you were alerted about the situation.

After the successful test you push the situation type to the production system.

Forward the Situation Type

Log-in as administrator and open the Import Collection app.

  • Select your software collection and a new page opens
  • On the header select Forward and wait until the validation is processed successfully
  • Then choose Forward on the bottom and wait until the export is finished successfully

Production System

Import Collection to Production System

As administrator open the Import Collection app on the production tenant in the production system.

  • Choose your software collection and a new page opens
  • On the header select Manual Import and wait until the check is processed successfully
  • Then choose Import on the bottom and wait until the import is finished successfully

Activate the Situation Type

Log-in as business process specialist and open the Manage Situation Types – Extended app. Like in the test system, you need to maintain the situation type.

  • Enable the situation type
  • Add the filter values for the trigger conditions
  • Ensure that at least one recipient team is enabled

Now you are all set.

Further Information

You find many more blog posts in the Best Practices section of Intelligent Situation Handling in the SAP Community. And you can visit SAP Help Portal for further guidance on Situation Handling.

entry.title: Converting to the 3-System Landscape – Part 5: Microlearning Videos
entry.content:
Co-Author: Dennis Leslie, Kate Wilson

Are you considering a move to the SAP Central Business Configuration–based SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, 3‑system landscape – but still unsure what to expect or how to get oriented? Good news: the following microlearning videos are available at no cost to guide you through the essentials.

  • Managing Transports in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape
    Microlearning video

     

    This video explains how different types of data are transported from the development system to the test and production systems. It outlines a structured and streamlined transport process designed to minimize risk within the 3‑system landscape.

  • Exploring the Test Automation Tool in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape
    Microlearning video
    This video highlights the features of the test automation tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The tool simplifies business process testing after release upgrades and is most effective in the 3-system landscape.

  • Navigating Parallel Line in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape
    Microlearning video
    This video explains the parallel line feature. A parallel line is created as a copy of your main line and provides an additional SAP Central Business Configuration workspace, along with an extra development and test tenant for various use cases. This no‑cost feature is exclusive to the 3‑system landscape.

  • Exploring Developer Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape
    Microlearning video
    This video highlights the three types of extensibility – key user, developer, and side‑by‑side. While key user and side‑by‑side extensibility are available in both 2‑ and 3‑system landscapes, developer extensibility is exclusive to the 3‑system landscape. Developer extensibility allows you to create customer‑specific extensions to released SAP objects using ABAP, enabling a deeper level of customization.

  • Using the Configuration Localization Tool in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape
    Microlearning video
    This video explains the features of the Configuration Localization Tool, which enables the creation of customer‑specific countries by localizing business configuration and is available exclusively in the 3‑system landscape.

Explore the power of the 3-system landscape today! To learn more, check out the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, 3-System Landscape page on SAP Community.

Looking for the earlier posts in this series? You'll find them here:

  • Converting to the 3-System Landscape – Part 1: What’s in It for You
  • Converting to the 3-System Landscape – Part 2: Making Science’s Success Story 
  • Converting to the 3-System Landscape – Part 3: Rapid Conversion Strategies
  • Converting to the 3-System Landscape – Part 4: How SAP Supports Your Transition

If you liked this blog, please leave us a thumbs up at the top. Also, make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition：为什么越来越多的客户开始使用 Parallel Line（并行线）？
entry.content:
随着企业对业务灵活性、快速创新与高质量上线的要求不断提升，SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition（SAP ERP公有云）在 2025 年正式引入了 Parallel Line（并行线） 的概念。这一能力自发布以来，迅速成为客户最关注的创新之一。截止作者发布此文为止，已有超过200家客户激活并开始使用了并行线功能。

         那么，“并行线”到底是什么？为什么企业迫切需要它？它能解决哪些痛点？希望这篇文章将带你完整理解其价值。

一、为什么需要 Parallel Line？——来自客户的真实声音

         长期以来，SAP ERP公有云的3系统架构（开发、测试、生产）虽然能满足核心实施项目的需要，但在企业实际操作中，经常出现以下诉求：

  • 想要单独的 数据迁移模拟环境
  • 想要并行 SIT/UAT 测试环境
  • 想要 长期稳定培训环境
  • 想要验证 新的第三方集成或新功能
  • 想要为 新增国家、组织结构提供独立配置空间
  • 想让 后续项目不影响当前主线或生产环境

         这些诉求在传统3系统架构中无法完全满足。

         因此，Parallel Line 并行线诞生了。

二、什么是 Parallel Line？

         Parallel Line（以下简称 PL）可以理解为：

“基于主线复制出的第二套配置与测试环境，用于扩展项目、迁移演练和集成测试，并且可选择将配置合并回主线。”

         并行线由以下组成：

  • CBC 内给PL单独的工作区（workspace）
  • 开发系统的并行定制租户（D120）
  • 测试系统的并行测试租户（T120）

         它是一条与主线平行存在的“支线”。

📘 并行线与主线的关系（图示）

三、Parallel Line 并行线能做什么？——典型应用场景

PL 的应用场景可分为两大类：

💡（A）仅需要 Branch（不需合并回主线）

这些场景仅需要复制出并行线，然后单独使用即可，例如：

  • 构建 多轮数据迁移测试
  • 多场景测试：SIT、UAT、回归测试
  • 构建第三方 集成测试环境
  • 提供长期稳定的 培训环境
  • 展示新功能、新的Scope Item等

以下是简化场景矩阵：

💡（B）使用PL后需要合并回主线

这些场景是指在PL并行线中完成相关的操作，然后把所做的更改合并回主线，例如：

  • 添加 新国家/地区
  • 扩展 新的组织架构
  • 增加新的范围项目（Scope item）
  • 增强产品特定配置

 以下是简化场景矩阵：

 

四、使用 Parallel Line并行线 的六大核心好处

 

1. 额外、独立的迁移 / 测试 / 培训环境

避免资源冲突，解决：

  • 测试之间互相覆盖
  • 培训占用测试环境
  • 数据迁移无法反复演练

2. 扩展性配置不影响主线/生产

例如：

  • 新增国家/地区
  • 新公司代码、工厂
  • 新本地化流程
  • 扩展 范围项目（Scope Item）

可以全部在 PL 中进行。

3. 提高数据迁移成功率

因为PL并行线环境可以反复刷新，所以数据迁移可在并行线环境中反复演练，保证上线质量。

4. 更强的集成验证能力

PL 是一套独立并且完整的系统，包含了除生产机之外的整套系统架构，可做：

  • SAP 内部集成验证
  • 第三方接口验证
  • 中间件流程测试

5. 稳定培训环境

         也有不少客户需要给新员工提供一个培训环境，PL并行线不影响生产、不被当前测试影响、同时也会跟随版本升级而升级，是一个作为培训的最佳环境。

**6. 最重要：**无需额外订阅费用

PL并行线环境包含在了客户3系统云订阅中，无需额外付费。客户可在SAP for Me里自助激活。

激活流程大致如下：

1. 在SAP for Me里，选择Service & Support，点击New Service Request：

 2. 选择Partner & Other Cloud Services，选择Create Service Request：

 3. 在Service Catalog中输入关键字Parallel，会找到并行线相关服务。Check Prerequisites服务用来检查是否有创建并行线的前提条件，Branch服务用来创建并行线。请首先选择Check Prerequisites服务，通过之后选择Branch服务：

 

五、并行线 vs 沙箱：业务场景差异

         在与一些客户的交流中，经常有一些客户会问到并行线系统与沙箱（sandbox）或者starter系统的区别，现把两者的区别整理如下：

 

一句话总结：

沙箱用于“看”，并行线用于“干”。

六、为什么并行线值得企业采用？

从业务管理层角度看，并行线的最大价值在于：

  • ✔ 提高上线成功率
  • ✔ 提供快速扩展能力
  • ✔ 降低创新风险
  • ✔ 分离配置波次，保证稳定性
  • ✔ 支撑全球化、多国家 rollout

来自使用过并行线功能的真实客户评价：

“我们真诚地认为，并行线环境已经，并且将继续对 SAP 项目的成功产生重大影响。”

“我们将并行线视为非常重要的创新，对未来新的实施至关重要。如果我们没有并行线，生产租户将面临风险。”

七、结语

         Parallel Line 并行线已经成为 S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 中最具战略价值的能力之一。无论是数据迁移、测试、培训，还是复杂的扩展配置，它都带来了前所未有的灵活性与安全性。

         如果你的企业正在：

  • 推全球化 rollout
  • 多批次上线
  • 多系统集成
  • 有复杂迁移场景

         并行线几乎是必备能力。

         如果您看了这篇文章，想了解更多关于Parallel Line并行线的内容，我也把几个非常有用的文档链接分享如下：

  • Parallel Line Overview文档
  • 并行线SAP Help Portal链接
  • Parallel Line FAQ

         当然，有任何问题也可以与我联系。感谢阅读！

entry.title: Enhancing Cost Center Security with Company Code Restrictions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

Security and access control are fundamental pillars of enterprise resource planning systems. As organizations grow and their data becomes increasingly sensitive, the need for granular authorization controls becomes paramount. SAP S/4HANA Cloud continues to evolve its authorization framework to meet these critical business requirements. For example: Authorization on company code level for app Manage Cost Centers F1443A

One significant enhancement to the authorization architecture is the introduction of Company Code restrictions based on company code. This new feature represents an important step forward in helping organizations maintain tighter control over who can access and manage cost center information across different business entities.

In this blog post, we explore this authorization enhancement, its implications for system administrators, and the steps necessary to ensure a smooth transition when this feature becomes active in upcoming versions(2608) of SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Scenario: Understanding the Business Need

The Challenge of Cost Center Access Control

Cost centers are critical organizational structures used for allocating costs, tracking expenses, and managing financial reporting across different departments and business units. In large organizations with multiple company codes (representing different legal entities or business divisions), the challenge of managing who can access which cost centers becomes increasingly complex.

Consider this real-world scenario:

A global manufacturing company operates multiple subsidiaries, each with its own company code:

- Company Code 1000 (Europe Division)

- Company Code 2000 (Asia Division)

- Company Code 3000 (Americas Division)

Each division has its own cost centers and financial managers. Previously, without granular Company Code restrictions, a user authorized for cost center management might inadvertently access cost center data across all company codes—potentially exposing sensitive financial information and creating compliance risks.

The Solution: Company Code Restrictions

The new Restricted Access to Cost Centers Based on Company Code feature addresses this security gap by enabling administrators to:

  1. Limit Cost Center Visibility: Restrict users to view only cost centers associated with their authorized company codes
  2. Control Modification Rights: Ensure that users can only modify cost center master data within their assigned company codes
  3. Enforce Compliance: Meet regulatory requirements for data segregation and access controls
  4. Improve Audit Trails: Create clear, auditable records of who accessed which cost centers

Implementation Details

 1.Authorization Requirements

To configure and manage Company Code restrictions, administrators must have specific authorization:

Required Business Catalog:

- `SAP_CORE_BC_IAM_RM` – This business catalog must be assigned to administrators responsible for identity and access management (IAM)

This ensures that only qualified IAM professionals can modify restriction configurations and prevent unauthorized changes to security settings.

 

2.Configuration Steps

Administrators must take proactive steps to configure the Company Code restriction before it becomes mandatory. Here's how:

Key Configuration Tool:

- App: Maintain Business Roles (F1492)

- Action: Preconfigure Company Code restriction values for all business roles that manage cost center master data

Configuration Process:

  1. Open the Maintain Business Roles app (F1492)
  2. Select the business role requiring Company Code restrictions
  3. Navigate to the restriction configuration section
  4. Assign appropriate company code values to the restriction
  5. Save and activate the configuration

Best Practice: Conduct this configuration during a low-usage period and test thoroughly in a sandbox environment before applying to production.

 

3. Affected Technical Objects

The Company Code restriction will impact access control across multiple layers of the SAP system:

CDS Views (Core Data Services):

- `I_CostCenter` – Main cost center master data view

- `I_CostCenterVH` – Cost center value help view

- `I_CostCenterSTDVH` – Cost center standard value help

- `I_CostCenterTP_2` – Cost center transactional posting view

Data Dictionary Value Help:

- `KOST` – Traditional value help for cost centers

Business Object Interface:

- `I_CostCenterTP_2` – Business object handling cost center transactions

 

4.Expected Changes in System Behavior

Once the Company Code restriction takes effect, the following changes will occur:

Access Restrictions:

- Users without proper Company Code authorization will no longer be able to view cost center master data outside their assigned company codes

- Attempts to access unauthorized cost centers will result in data being unavailable

- System actions involving cost center selection will filter options based on Company Code authorization

Impact on User Experience:

- Value help dropdowns (F4 help) will display only authorized cost centers

- Reports and analytics will show only data relevant to assigned company codes

- Cost center searches will return filtered results

Administrative Impact:

- Administrators must ensure all affected business roles have Company Code restrictions configured

- Failure to configure restrictions may result in users losing access to previously available data

 Configuration Timeline and Recommendations

 Phase 1: Immediate Action (Current)

- Review current authorization setup for cost center management

- Identify all business roles that handle cost center master data

- Audit current user access patterns to establish baseline data

 Phase 2: Pre-configuration (Before Upgrade)

- Configure Company Code restrictions using the Maintain Business Roles app (F1492)

- Assign appropriate company code values to each business role

- Test configuration in development/sandbox environment

 Phase 3: Validation and Testing

- Conduct end-to-end testing with representative users from each company code

- Verify that users can access only authorized cost centers

- Test value help, reports, and transaction access

- Document any access issues or unexpected behaviors

 Phase 4: Deployment (Upon Release)

- Deploy the update to production systems

- Monitor system performance and user feedback

- Address any access issues promptly

- Maintain documentation of changes

For additional information about this enhancement:

- SAP Note 3632368 - Restricted Access to Cost Centers Based on Company Code - SAP for Me

- SAP Documentation: Restricted Access to Cost Centers Based on Company Code | SAP Help Portal

 Conclusion

The introduction of Company Code restrictions in cost center master data management represents a meaningful advancement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud's authorization capabilities. By enabling organizations to enforce granular access controls based on company code assignments, this feature enhances security, improves compliance posture, and reduces the risk of unintended data exposure.

Key Takeaways:

  1. Proactive action is Essential: Don't wait until the feature is mandatory to configure Company Code restrictions. Preconfigure values now using the Maintain Business Roles app.
  2. Comprehensive Planning Required: Audit your current authorization structure, identify affected business roles, and plan your configuration strategy in advance.
  3. Testing is Critical: Thoroughly test the configuration in non-production environments before deploying to production to prevent user access issues.
  4. Administrative Oversight: Ensure that administrators with appropriate authorization (SAP_CORE_BC_IAM_RM) manage these configurations to maintain security.
  5. Ongoing Compliance: Regularly review and maintain Company Code restrictions as organizational structures and user roles evolve.

For organizations committed to maintaining robust security controls and regulatory compliance, this enhancement aligns perfectly with best practices in identity and access management. By taking a structured, well-planned approach to implementation, administrators can ensure a seamless transition when the Company Code restriction becomes mandatory in upcoming SAP S/4HANA Cloud versions.

The time to act is now. Begin your pre-configuration efforts today to ensure business continuity and security when this critical authorization enhancement takes effect.

Next Steps:

- Schedule a review of your current cost center authorization structure

- Identify business roles requiring Company Code restriction configuration

- Plan your implementation timeline to align with upcoming system updates

- Contact your SAP system administrators to begin pre-configuration efforts

entry.title: “Custom attributes” in ABAP platform workflows and related inboxes
entry.content:
SAP Business Workflow was first introduced in 1995 as part of SAP R/3 Release 3.0 (see: SAP R/3: Architecture, Function, Implementation" by Buck-Emden et.al..)

This longevity speaks to the robustness and deep integration of the tool in the ABAP platform – why also often the name ABAP platform workflow is used. A main goal was and is to distribute workitems (often also called “to dos” or “tasks” – don´t mix up this runtime artefact with the task-object from design time [e.g. TS30000016]) to the next worker to accomplish business outcomes in a coordinated way and as effortless as possible for human workers interacting with a digital system. Same is true for compliance approval processes implemented via flexible workflows in SAP S/4HANA CLOUD - we celebrate 10th Birthday this year.

Almost from the beginning, customizable task attributes and personalized worklist searching & sorting have been essential for enabling end-users to complete their work with freedom and flexibility—allowing them to work in the sequence their company or business process requires. This directly addresses all three psychological needs that Self-Determination Theory identifies as drivers of intrinsic motivation and self-actualization: autonomy, competence, and social relatedness. The concept of “custom attributes”: Company and business process-specific metadata that enables:

  • Task filtering and prioritization
  • Personalized worklist management
  • Context-aware task processing

This blog post deals mainly with the latest technology to define CDS Entity based custom attributes (which is not covered in the workflow overview training BIT602). For the sake of completeness, the older options are also mentioned or linked.

31 and also 10 years are tremendous for software components and tools. Congratulations to all contributed to that amazing story.

CDS Entity based custom attributes for My Inbox and SAP Task Center

CDS Entity-based custom attributes are the recommended approach for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and available sind ABAP Platform 2023. The CDS Entity must have identical key fields as the leading object associated with the workflow task or scenario. This method is supported for flexible workflow scenarios and their steps only.

Prerequisites Checklist

✓ Flexible workflow scenario with attached leading object

✓ CDS Entity key fields match leading object keys

✓ CDS Entity has @Workflow: { enabledFor: [ #CUSTOM_TASK_ATTRIBUTES ] } * key user possible [see section "Custom Attributes via key user extensibility" below]

✓ For amount/quantity fields: annotations include @Semantics

✓ Maximum 30 attributes per step (Task Center limitation)

Development

Note that it will only be possible to use the CDS based configuration for Tasks or Scenarios with an attached leading object.

At runtime, the system uses the CDS Entity name and field to populate custom attribute values. Configuration varies by deployment model:

  • SWF_USER_ATTR_DEF: Application development
  • SWF_USER_ATTR_CUST: Partner/OP customer development
  • SWF_USER_ATTR_CDS: Cloud customers (via SSCUI)

Note that the chosen CDS Entity has to have the annotation @Workflow: { enabledFor: [ #CUSTOM_TASK_ATTRIBUTES ] } if used in the transactions SWF_USER_ATTR_DEF, SWF_USER_ATTR_CUST or SWF_USER_ATTR_CDS. 

If the CDS entity field chosen is an amount or value field and carries one of the following annotations '@Semantics.amount.currencyCode' or '@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure' the corresponding currency or unit information will be additionally exposed to the frontend. The label of the corresponding column would in this case be taken from the '@EndUserText.label' given in the CDS Entity'.

Note that the annotation referencing the currency or unit of measure is evaluated during definition of custom attribute. This means that if any changes are done to those annotations in the CDS view after the custom attribute was maintained these changes will not be evaluated anymore. If such changes are done the custom attribute should be removed and recreated.

Custom Attributes Definitions created in SWF_USER_ATTR_DEF are not displayed in the transaction SWF_USER_ATTR_CDS but can be redefined using the button.

Definition and customization

SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition provides three transactions for viewing, defining, and modifying CDS-based custom attributes:

Custom Attributes: Definition and Customization

Transaction Types

  1. SWF_USER_ATTR_DEF

 Purpose: Define custom attributes across all clients

  • Use Case: Typically used for solution delivery (e.g., by SAP Partners)
  • Transport Method: Workbench request
  1. SWF_USER_ATTR_CUST

Purpose: Define custom attributes for a specific client

  • Capabilities:
    • Define new custom attributes
    • Overwrite definitions from SWF_USER_ATTR_DEF
  • Transport Method: Customizing transport
  1. SWF_USER_ATTR_CDS

Purpose: Enable cloud customers to define or modify custom attributes

  • Availability:
    • SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition (full transaction access)
    • SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition (via SSCUI - CDS view definition only) available from 2402 onwards over "Configure Your Solution"
  • Key Features:
    • CDS-based definition capability
    • "Convert into CDS based cust. Attribute" button to migrate from task container definitions
    • "Copy Entry from System Settings" button to replicate existing definitions

Important Considerations

Aspect

Details

CDS Definition Restrictions

Only allowed if no task container definition exists

Conversion Impact

Converting from Expression to CDS removes the original definition and cannot be reverted by cloud customers

Already Redefined Attributes

Do not appear in the selection screen when copying

Maximum Limit

30 custom attributes per workflow step (Task Center service limitation)

Recommendation

Define maximum 30 custom attributes per step

Granularity Options

Task Level

  • Expose the same custom attributes across all workflow steps using that task
  • Example: Task TS78500059

Workflow Step Level

  • Expose custom attributes for a specific workflow step (identified by Step ID)
  • Example: Task WS02000006, Step ID 1
  • Benefit: Useful when a task is reused differently (e.g., different approval perspectives)

Configuration Fields

Field

Description

Notes

Use Case

Groups or filters user attributes

Free text (max. 20 characters)

Name of Attribute

Unique identifier

Special identifiers starting with "custom" display in specific inbox locations

Description

Identifying text

Max. 40 characters; translatable via ABAP translation mechanism

Rank

Priority/importance indicator

Higher rank = higher importance; affects display order in Task Center

CDS Entity

Source data entity name

(CDS-based only) Must have same key as the task's leading object

CDS Entity Field

Specific field containing display data

(CDS-based only)

Expression

Container element reference

(Container-based only) Use format: &ELEMENT_NAME&; supports combined values

Type

ABAP data type

(Container-based only)

Field

External field type

(Container-based only)

Screenshots

 

 

Custom Attributes via key user extensibility 

WIth SAP S/4HANA Cloud , public edition 2505 and 2025 private edition key users can define custom attributes through the SSCUI in the Manage Your Solution app. If additional CDS Views are needed as a basis for their definition, you can use App Custom CDS Views to generate a CDS View by selecting the Custom CDS Scenario "Custom Attributes for Task." See detailed documentation. Note that CDS views generated this way will appear in the value help within the SSCUI. However, you must ensure that the key fields of the generated CDS view match the key fields of the workflow task's or workflow scenario's leading object. Additionally, the transport containing the generated CDS view should be released either before or together with the transport containing the SSCUI configuration.

Special Attributes in Custom Attributes Configuration

This part explains how to create complex custom attributes (with relations available with ABAP Platform Cloud 2402 /ABAP Platform 2025) beyond simple transaction-maintained ones:

Three Types of Special Attributes

Type

Annotation

Use Case

Value + Unit of Measure

@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure

Quantities with UOM (e.g., 10 KG)

Value + Currency

@Semantics.amount.currencyCode

Monetary amounts with currency (e.g., 100 USD)

Value + Description

@ObjectModel.text.element

Human-readable text pairs (e.g., Code + Description)

Please note, these related attributes are not available for My Inbox.

Key Requirements

CDS Entity Configuration

  • All referenced fields must be in the same CDS Entity, OR
  • Fields must be connected via an association with a single "on" condition

Text Arrangement (for Value + Description available from ABAP Platform Cloud 2405 / ABAP Platform 2025)

  • Use @ui.textArrangement: annotation to define display order
  • Default behavior: #TEXT_FIRST (description shown before value)

Language Considerations

Two Categories:

  1. Language Dependent
    • Example: itemCurrency, itemUOM
    • Evaluated per user's logon language
  2. Language Independent
    • Example: documentType, documentCategory
    • Same value across all languages

Language Management

  • Configuration Table: SWFCLANG (view: SWFCLANG_V)
  • User for Evaluation: SAP_WFRT logon language
  • Maintenance:
    • Private Cloud: SM30 transaction
    • Public Cloud: Configure Your Solution application
  • Storage: All texts stored with language keys per translation vector

 

Transport

Annotations and attribute configurations are transported together in the same transport request.

Sample CDS Entity and Custom Attributes

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED

@EndUserText.label: 'WF Demo: Leave Request Base (CDS)'

@ObjectModel.supportedCapabilities: [ #CDS_MODELING_ASSOCIATION_TARGET ]

@Workflow.enabledFor: [ #CUSTOM_TASK_ATTRIBUTES ]

define root view entity ZDK_WORKFLOWLEAVEREQUEST2
  as select from swxformabs

  association [0..1] to ZDK_DEMO_I_LR_TYPETEXT as _Text     on $projection.holidaytp1 = _Text.Type and _Text.Language = 'E'
  association [0..*] to I_Currency             as _Currency on $projection.Currency = _Currency.Currency

{
  key swxformabs.formnumber  as WorkflowLeaveRequest,

      swxformabs.approvby    as WFLeaveApprovedBy,
      swxformabs.createdby   as WFCreatedBy,

      swxformabs.addrnumber  as WFCreatorAddressNumber,
      swxformabs.pernr       as WFCreatorPersonalNumber,
      swxformabs.name        as WFCreatorNameText,
      swxformabs.department  as WFCreatorDepartment,
      swxformabs.costcenter  as WFCreatorCostcenter,

      swxformabs.firstday1   as WFLeavePartOneFirstDay,
      swxformabs.lastday1    as WFLeavePartOneLastDay,

      @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_Text'
      @ObjectModel.text.element: [ '_Text.TypeText' ]
      swxformabs.holidaytp1,
      @EndUserText.label: 'LeaveRequest Type'
      _Text.TypeText         as LeaveType,
      swxformabs.reason      as WFLeaveReason,
      swxformabs.contact     as WFLeaveContact,
      swxformabs.procstate   as WFLeaveProcessState,

      case swxformabs.procstate
        when 'A' then 'Approved'
        when 'R' then 'Rejected'
        else 'New'
      end                    as WFLeaveProcessStateText,

      @EndUserText.label: 'LeaveRequest Currency'
      @ObjectModel.text.element: [ '_Currency._Text.CurrencyName' ]
      @UI.textArrangement: #TEXT_LAST
      case when swxformabs.holidaytp1 = 'R' then 'EUR'
           else 'USD'
      end                    as Currency,

      _Currency,
      _Text
}

 

Custom Attribute Types and Database Storage

Virtual Element-Based Custom Attributes

These attributes derive values from virtual CDS elements. The database record stores an exit-class name in the EXIT_CLASS field, which is used to calculate the attribute values.

Text Reference Custom Attributes

This configuration uses two database entries:

  • A primary custom attribute with REF_ATTRIBUTE_NAME pointing to a text reference attribute
  • A text reference custom attribute that provides the actual values
Currency-Based Custom Attributes

This structure requires three database entries arranged hierarchically:

  • Primary custom attribute (references a currency attribute)
  • Currency reference attribute (references a text attribute)
  • Text reference attribute (base values)
Unit of Measure Custom Attributes

Similar to currency attributes, this configuration also uses three entries:

Primary custom attribute (references a unit of measure attribute)

Unit of measure reference attribute (references a text attribute)

Text reference attribute (base values)

Implementation Considerations
Virtual CDS Elements

While virtual CDS elements are technically supported for custom attribute definitions, their use is not recommended for performance and maintenance reasons.

Runtime Behavior
Data Availability and Timing

Custom attribute values are calculated and stored when work items are created. However, not all container elements may be available at creation time, and CDS entity data retrieved at this point may differ from real-time values. Post-execution values and UI-updated attributes may not exist during creation, making them unsuitable for inbox display.

Dynamic Updates

Developers can update (available since ABAP Platform Cloud 2505 / ABAP Platform 2025) custom attributes post-creation using the CL_SWF_UTL_RUN_USR_ATTR_CHG utility class. After populating a local buffer with work items (via append_business_obj, append_workflow, or append_workitems), calling request_custom_attribute_chg asynchronously updates all non-final work items within that workflow

DATA(lo_util) = cl_swf_utl_run_usr_attr_chg=>get_instance( )
lo_util->append_workflow( '<workflow id>' ).
lo_util->request_custom_attribute_chg( ).

or business object.

DATA(lo_util) = cl_swf_utl_run_usr_attr_chg=>get_instance( ).

lo_util->append_business_obj( VALUE #( catid  = '<category_id>'
                                       typeid = '<type_id>'
                                       instid = '<business_object_instance_id>' ) ).
lo_util->request_custom_attribute_chg( ).

Performance recommendations

  • Include texts in CDS entities: Text fields should be incorporated directly into CDS entities used for customer attribute definitions (as shown in the example above). This should be documented.
  • Minimize CDS entities: Keep the number of CDS entities used for customer attribute definitions as small as possible—ideally just one. This should be clearly documented.
  • Push domain and reference table texts to database level: If texts from domain values or reference tables are needed, associate them at the database level by linking text associations to the CDS entity that collects these texts. Refer to `I_WorkflowStatusText` as a reference example.
  • Enable full buffering for text tables: Text tables should be configured for full buffering whenever possible.
  • Avoid CDS Virtual Elements: Virtual elements should not be used for customer attribute definitions. If a virtual element must be used, implement the `GET_CALCULATION_INFO` method to specify which CDS entity elements are required for data calculation.

My inbox

How custom attributes are displayed and consumed depends on the view of My Inbox that is being used.

Master-Detail View

The Master-Detail View is the standard view of My Inbox in ABAP Platform. See further details here: Master-Detail View.

When using some predefined names in the definition of custom attributes, some labels in the My Inbox UI can be replaced with the exposed data.

You can find further details in this documentation: Display Custom Attributes in the List View.

Screenshot.

Since ABAP Platform 2102 and ABAP Platform 2021, when performing a mass action (decision), custom attributes are exposed to the user in the multi-selection view. The table in the multi-selection view can be sorted by the custom attributes and also be personalized.

Screenshot

Expert View

The Expert View offers a tabular representation of the standard and the exposed custom attributes of the workflow tasks. The attributes are displayed as columns that can be personalized, sorted, grouped and filtered.

See further details here: Expert View.

SAP Task Center

For tasks to be available in SAP Task Center via the integration also, the defined custom attributes will be exposed to Task Center and be persisted together with the standard attributes in the cache of the Task Center Service on the SAP Business Technology Platform.

The cache of SAP Task Center task is then used by the UIs (e.g. the Web App and SAP Mobile Start) without having to additionally call the task providers. How the custom attributes are shown depends on the connected UI. 

Container based custom attributes

The second option defining custom attributes is to use a reference to particular elements of the workflow container.
Once the task is created they are stored as name-value-pair in the database. As opposed to the container, which is commonly stored as XML, custom attributes can be read via a simple database select.

Following elements can be used:

  • Plain elements
  • Table of plain elements
  • Object attributes (which are plain elements or a table of plain elements) 

Structure elements are not supported. Only simple fields of the structure can be used. The supported data types are:

C

Character string

N

Character string with digits only

D

Date (YYYYMMDD)

T

Time (HHMMSS)

I

Integer number (4-byte integer with sign)

b

1-byte integer, whole number <= 254

s

2-byte integer, only for length field before LCHR or LRAW

P

Packed number

F

Floating point number to accuracy of 8 bytes

g

Character string with variable length (ABAP type STRING)

 

How custom attributes can be defined depends on the UI in which they should be shown.

Please check these two blogs for more details:

1. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/embellishing-your-approvals-without-coding-my-inbox/ba-p/13323357

2. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-fiori-my-inbox-2-0-sp02-features/ba-p/13228953

Exit based custom attributes for My Inbox via Task Gateway Configuration

In general the definition of custom attributes via the configuration in SAP Business Workflow should be preferred to the configuration within Task Gateway since:

Activating a BAdI implementation for a task definition invalidates the definitions and customizations that were done for that task definition in SAP Business Workflow. By that preventing extensions by customers.

Custom attributes that were defined by means of this BAdI will not be exposed to Task Center.

While strongly discouraged custom attributes can still provided at task instance level by implementing the /IWWRK/BADI_TGW_CUSTOM_ATTR BAdI in the /IWWRK/ES_TGW_CUSTOM_ATTRIBUTE enhancement spot. You can find more information here: Custom Attributes. 

 

History: dynamic columns in SAP Business Workplace

For Business Workflow it is possible to create custom columns.

Up to 12 custom columns can be defined using the transaction SWL1 or the menu path  Tools → Business Workflow → Development → Definition Tools → Worklist Client → Dynamic Columns for Worklist.

the following fields can be edited:

  1. User Name: Relevant user name or ‘*’ to support all users
  2. Task : Relevant task type
  3. Expression: Value that should be displayed in the UI.  As source a container element should be specified via its name enclosed into the ‘&’ character. Please ensure that the container element name is taken from the relevant task type, for example '&NAME&'
  4. Header: Label that should be displayed in the UI.

 

Please check these blogs:

1. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/sbwp-customization-tip-for-inbox-customization/ba-p/13304850

2. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/display-dynamic-columns-for-business-workplace-in-the-uwl/ba-p/1288610

Thanks to all contributors to this features & blog.

entry.title: 通过 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 中的公司代码限制增强成本中心安全性
entry.content:
简介

安全和访问控制是ERP系统的基本支柱。随着组织不断发展且数据越来越敏感，对精细权限控制的需求变得至关重要。SAP S/4HANA Cloud 继续发展其权限框架，以满足这些关键业务需求。例如: Authorization on company code level for app Manage Cost Centers F1443A

权限体系结构的一个重要增强是引入基于公司代码的公司代码限制。此新功能代表向前迈出的重要一步，有助于组织更严格地控制可以跨不同业务实体访问和管理成本中心信息的人员。

在此博客中，我们将探索此权限增强、其对系统管理员的影响以及确保此功能在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 即将发布的版本 (2608) 中处于激活状态时实现平稳转换所需的步骤。

场景：了解业务需求

成本中心访问控制的挑战

成本中心是用于在不同部门和业务单位之间分配成本、跟踪费用以及管理财务报表的关键组织结构。在具有多个公司代码的大型组织（代表不同的法律实体或业务部门）中，管理谁可以访问哪些成本中心变得越来越复杂。

考虑以下真实场景：

一家全球制造公司经营多个子公司，每个子公司都有自己的公司代码：

- 公司代码 1000（欧洲分部）

- 公司代码 2000（亚洲分部）

- 公司代码 3000（美洲分部）

每个部门都有自己的成本中心和财务经理。之前，在没有详细的公司代码限制的情况下，有权进行成本中心管理的用户可能会无意中访问所有公司代码的成本中心数据 - 可能会暴露敏感财务信息并产生合规性风险。

解决方案：为成本中心部署公司代码限制

新的基于公司代码的成本中心受限访问功能使管理员能够：

1.限制成本中心可见性：限制用户仅查看与其授权公司代码相关联的成本中心

2.控制修改权限：确保用户只能修改其已分配公司代码中的成本中心主数据

3.加强合规性：满足数据分离和访问控制方面的法规要求

4.改进审计跟踪：创建明确、可审计的记录，提供谁访问哪些成本中心的信息

实施详细信息

  1. 权限要求

要配置和管理公司代码限制，管理员必须具有特定权限：

所需业务目录：

- `SAP_CORE_BC_IAM_RM` - 此业务目录必须分配给负责身份和访问管理 (IAM) 的管理员

这可确保只有合格的 IAM 专业人员才能修改限制配置并防止对安全设置进行未经授权的更改。

 

  1.  配置步骤

管理员必须采取主动步骤来配置公司代码限制，然后才能成为强制限制。方法如下：

关键配置工具：

- 应用：维护业务角色 (F1492)

- 操作：为管理成本中心主数据的所有业务角色预配置公司代码限制值

配置流程：

1.打开维护业务角色应用 (F1492)

2.选择需要公司代码限制的业务角色

3.导航到限制配置部分

4.为限制分配相应的公司代码值

  1. 保存并激活配置

最佳实践：请在系统的低使用期间执行此配置，并在应用于生产系统之前在非生产环境中全面测试。

  1. 受影响的技术对象

公司代码限制将影响 SAP 系统多个层的访问控制：

CDS 视图（核心数据服务）：

- `I_CostCenter` – 主要成本中心主数据视图

- `I_CostCenterVH` – 成本中心值帮助视图

- `I_CostCenterSTDVH` – 成本中心标准值帮助

- `I_CostCenterTP_2` – 成本中心交易过账视图

数据字典值帮助：

- `KOST` – 成本中心的传统值帮助

业务对象接口：

- `I_CostCenterTP_2` – 业务对象处理成本中心事务

  1. 系统行为的预期更改

公司代码限制生效后，将发生以下更改：

访问限制：

- 不具备适当公司代码权限的用户将无法再查看已分配公司代码之外的成本中心主数据

- 尝试访问未经授权的成本中心将导致数据不可用

- 涉及成本中心选择的系统操作将根据公司代码权限过滤选项

对用户体验的影响：

- 值帮助下拉列表（F4 帮助）将仅显示已授权的成本中心

- 报表和分析将仅显示与已分配公司代码相关的数据

- 成本中心搜索将返回过滤结果

管理影响：

- 管理员必须确保所有受影响的业务角色都已配置公司代码限制

- 配置限制失败可能导致用户对之前可用数据的访问权限丢失

 配置时间表和建议

 第1阶段：立即操作（当前）

- 检查成本中心管理的当前权限设置

- 识别处理成本中心主数据的所有业务角色

— 审计当前用户访问模式以建立基本数据

 

 第 2 阶段：预配置（升级前）

- 使用维护业务角色应用 (F1492) 配置公司代码限制

- 为每个业务角色分配相应的公司代码值

- 开发/沙盒环境中的测试配置

 

 第 3 阶段：验证和测试

- 与每个公司代码的代表用户执行端到端测试

- 验证用户是否只能访问授权的成本中心

- 测试值帮助、报表和事务访问

— 记录任何权限访问的问题或意外行为

 

 第 4 阶段：部署（发布时）

- 将更新部署到生产系统

- 监控系统性能和用户反馈

- 及时解决任何访问问题

- 维护变更文档

 

有关此增强的附加信息：

- SAP 注释3632368 - Restricted Access to Cost Centers Based on Company Code - SAP for Me

- SAP 文档: Restricted Access to Cost Centers Based on Company Code | SAP Help Portal

 结论

成本中心主数据管理中引入公司代码限制是 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 权限功能的很有意义进步。使组织能够基于公司代码分配实施精细访问控制，此功能可以增强安全性、改进合规性并降低意外数据暴露的风险。

 

关键要点：

1.主动操作至关重要：不要等到配置公司代码限制时必须使用此功能。现在使用维护业务角色应用预配置值。

2.需要全面计划：审计当前权限结构，识别受影响的业务角色，并提前计划配置策略。

3.测试至关重要：在部署到生产之前彻底测试非生产环境中的配置，以防止用户访问问题。

4.管理监督：确保具有相应权限 (SAP_CORE_BC_IAM_RM) 的管理员管理这些配置以维护安全性。

5.持续合规：随着组织结构和用户角色的发展，定期审核和维护公司代码限制。

对于致力于保持强大的安全控制和法规合规性的组织，此增强完全符合身份和访问管理的最佳实践。通过采用结构化、计划良好的实施方法，管理员可以在即将到来的 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 版本中强制实施公司代码限制时确保无缝过渡。

 

现在是行动的时候了。立即开始预配置工作，以确保此关键权限增强生效时的业务连续性和安全性。

后续步骤：

- 计划检查当前成本中心权限结构

- 识别需要公司代码限制配置的业务角色

- 规划实施时间表，与即将进行的系统更新保持一致

- 联系 SAP 系统管理员，开始预配置工作

entry.title: Influence Tomorrow’s ERP Technology with SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1-2026
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA is transforming the way businesses operate. By delivering intelligent automation, real-time analytics, and seamless integration across processes, it enables organizations to drive efficiency, agility, and innovation at scale. Now, you can help shape the future of enterprise resource planning.

Cycle 1 of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2026 is still open, offering SAP customers and partners the unique opportunity to directly influence the evolution of SAP S/4HANA. Collaborate with SAP product teams, share your insights early, and contribute to the development of new features and enhancements.

This is more than just a preview – it’s your chance to play a pivotal role in shaping the future of intelligent ERP solutions.

Registration is open until March 13, 2026. 

Check out the full list here or follow the links below to discover the exciting opportunities:

ERP Cloud Delivery & Excellence 

  • A modern Output Management experience in ERP Suite
  • AI Assisted SAP Workflow Management
  • AI assisted use cases for Embedded Analytics
  • Business Partner and Product Maser in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Master Data Analytics Dashboard
  • Task Center (One Inbox) experience evaluation in SAP Business Suite

ERP - Cross Topics

  • Farmer Collaboration and Engagement

ERP Finance & Risk

  • Improving Sanction Party List screening with AI
  • Limit Management Modernization (Credit Risk Analyzer)
  • Simplify and Improve the Intrastat Declaration Process with AI
  • Unlock more flexible Margin Analysis in SAP Cloud ERP

ERP Private Cloud

  • Embedded AI and Joule for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • E-Mails in business context with integrated business communications for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

ERP Public Cloud

  • Agentic AI – Analytical Business Insights
  • Agentic AI Smart Billing Assistant
  • AI Agent with Financial Correspondences
  • Concept for Harmonized Period Control
  • Customer returns user experience
  • Configuration experience of Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Enhanced Payment Order Management in Automatic Payment Run Process
  • Identity and Access Management: Upgrade and Change Handling
  • IFRS 9- Credit-Risk Based Impairment for Trade Receivables using Advanced Valuation
  • Generate Settlement Rules
  • Parallel Unit of Measure
  • Project staffing, consider dependencies & provide smart staffing across different roles of projects

Let’s shape the future together!

Innovation happens through collaboration. By participating in the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects, you help SAP create better, more relevant solutions for businesses like yours. This is your chance to influence what comes next.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2026!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Creating a DQM Validation Rule for Business Partner Role Assignment in SAP MDG
entry.content:
Overview

Data quality is critical in Master Data Governance (MDG), especially when dealing with Business Partners. One common data quality issue occurs when Business Partners are created without any Business Partner Role assigned. Since roles determine how a Business Partner participates in business processes (customer, supplier, etc.), missing roles can break downstream applications.

The goal is to create a DQM validation rule in SAP Master Data Governance that prevents the creation or modification of Business Partners without role assignments. The system will show a validation error or warning as shown in the following screenshot:

The validation logic works as follows:

  1. The rule iterates over Business Partner records in table BUT000.
  2. For each Business Partner ID, a table operation checks table BUT100.
  3. If no entry exists in BUT100 for that BP, the validation rule raises an error.

This ensures that every Business Partner has at least one role assigned before it can be saved.

Prerequisites

Before implementing the rule, ensure the following requirements are met:

  • SAP Master Data Governance with Data Quality Management (DQM) enabled
  • User authorization with the SAP_BR_BUPA_MASTER_STEWARD role or equivalent
  • Access to the SAP Fiori application Validation Rules for Business Partners (App IDs F4084 or F4082A (newer releases)

Rule Configuration

Step 1 — Access the DQM Validation App

  1. Open the SAP Fiori Launchpad.
  2. Launch the Validation Rules for Business Partners app.

This UI/App allows you to manage and create validation rules for Business Partner data

Step 2 — Create a New Validation Rule

Click Create and enter the following information:

Field

Value

Rule ID

CHECK_BP_ROLE_MANDATORY

Rule Name

BP Role Assignment Required

Base Table

BUT000 (Business Partner General Data)

Checked Field

BUT0BK-PARTNER_ROLE (optional but recommended)

The base table must be BUT000, even though the validation checks data stored in another table

Step 3 — Maintain General Information

Fill in the business metadata for the rule:

Description
Ensures every Business Partner has at least one role assigned.

Reason
Business Partners without roles cannot participate in business processes.

Rule Owner
Data Steward responsible for Business Partner data.

Implementation Expert
Technical contact responsible for BRF+ implementation.

Save the rule by clicking Create.

Step 4 — Technical Implementation

Configure Rule Usage

  1. In the Usage section, select Add.
  2. Choose Check in Central Governance.
  3. Click Prepare to generate the BRF+ application.
  4. Select Send for Implementation.

This enables the rule logic to be edited.

Scope Expression Configuration

The Scope Expression defines which Business Partners should be validated.

Steps:

  1. Open the Scope Expression link.
  2. The Simplified BRF+ Workbench appears.
  3. Define the scope.

Example scope:

Partner Category = 2 (Organization)

This means the validation rule will only apply to organizational Business Partners.

You can also configure the rule to apply to all Business Partners.

Condition Expression Implementation

The main validation logic is defined in the Condition Expression.

Steps:

  1. Open the Condition Expression.
  2. Remove the dummy condition.
  3. Create a new expression.

Choose:

Expression Type: Table Operation

Example name: BUT100_CHECK

Table Operation Configuration

Configure the operation as follows:

Parameter

Value

Operation

Has at least

Table

Procedure Call

Procedure

BUT0BK lookup ALL

Condition

Partner Role is not initial

This configuration checks if at least one role exists for the Business Partner.

 

Although the rule loops over BUT000, the lookup ultimately checks role entries in BUT100.

This cross-table validation is the key technical detail of this solution.

Activation and Deployment

Once testing is complete:

  1. Select Send for Testing to change the rule status.
  2. After validation, choose Approve.
  3. Go to the Usage tab.
  4. Click Enable for Check in Central Governance.

The rule is now active and will validate Business Partner data during governance processes.

Testing the Rule

Test the validation logic.

Test Scenarios

Scenario

Expected Result

BP without roles

Validation error

BP with one role

Validation passes

BP with multiple roles

Validation passes

Testing ensures that only valid Business Partner records can be saved.

 

Troubleshooting

Rule Not Triggering

Check the following:

  • All BRF+ expressions are active
  • Rule status is Approved
  • Usage is Enabled

Performance Issues

If validation becomes slow:

  • Restrict the Scope Expression
  • Limit evaluation to specific Business Partner categories
  • Avoid unnecessary table operations

 

Best Regards

Steffen

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition certifications updated to release 2602
entry.content:
We’re excited to share that all SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition certification exams have now been updated to reflect the features, enhancements, and innovations delivered with the 2602 release.

This update ensures that learners, consultants, and implementation teams continue to validate their skills based on the most current capabilities of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

What This Means for You

1. Exams Aligned with the Latest Release

All learning journeys, courses, practice systems, and system-based exams have been refreshed to align with the 2602 product release. Learners preparing for an exam can expect content that reflects the newest system behavior, UI updates, functional enhancements, and configuration options.

2. Updated Learning Resources

To support learners in their preparation, corresponding SAP Learning content has also been updated. You’ll now find:

  • Revised learning journeys and course material
  • Updated hands-on exercises where applicable
  • New or updated learning system environments reflecting 2602

3. Smooth Transition for Ongoing Learners

If you were already preparing for a certification, you can continue studying without disruption. Updated materials are available immediately, and exam codes remain unchanged unless otherwise communicated in the individual learning journey.

4. Stay Current with SAP Innovations

SAP’s commitment to continuous enablement ensures that certifications stay relevant and reflect the latest release cycles. By certifying on 2602, you demonstrate knowledge and readiness aligned with SAP’s most recent innovations.

What You Should Do Next

Visit the updated learning journeys to access the updated materials:

    • Applying End-to-End Business Processes in SAP Business Suite
    • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
    • Implementing Financial Accounting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
    • Implementing Management Accounting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
    • Implementing Sales in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
    • Implementing Sourcing and Procurement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Schedule your certification exam when you’re ready—you’ll now be tested on 2602 content.

entry.title: Custom Adobe Forms in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud using Developer Extensibility
entry.content:
Introduction

  • Custom Adobe forms can be created in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud using Developer Extensibility using Form Objects.
  • OData Service can be created to get Form details.

Process to Develop Custom Adobe Forms in SAP S4HANA Public Cloud using Developer Extensibility

Forms Objects

  • Creating Forms Objects in the Form editor allows for the generation of print and interactive forms using Adobe Document Services.
  • Forms Objects can be created in the Form editor.
  • Print and interactive forms can be generated using Adobe Document Services (ADS) by the Forms Object.
  • The main part of Forms Object is layout form design file (XDP format by Adobe).
  • Adobe LiveCycle Designer is required for the development of XDP layout file.

Creating Form Objects

  • A Form Object can be created in ABAP development tools for Eclipse (ADT) using the creation wizard

Print Forms

ADS Rendering in ABAP environment

  • In the ABAP environment with Adobe Document Services (ADS), rendering refers to the process of taking an Adobe XML Form (XFA) which is essentially a structured template containing layout, design, and data placeholders and producing a final output document.
  • The Adobe Document Services (ADS) can render Adobe XML Forms (XFA) to PDF documents and other print formats.
  • The application sends the data and the form template using a secure connection and the service returns the rendered document.
  • The rendered documents can be used by the applications for the screen output or for the output into the print queue.

Retrieve Stored Form Templates

Form templates designed with the Adobe LiveCycle Designer can be uploaded directly to the system using ABAP development tools for Eclipse.

The class CL_FP_FORM_READER enables the usage of uploaded form templates during runtime.

Initiate the Form Template Reader

Initiate the class by providing the name of the uploaded form template.

data(lo_reader) = cl_fp_form_reader=>create_form_reader( 'MY_FORM_NAME' ).

Read Layout Content to be Used by ADS Rendering Call

The layout can also be retrieved by the reader class.

The GET_LAYOUT method has the returning parameter RV_LAYOUT, it returns XML as XSTRING

RAP Data Services for Print Forms

  • The XML data for print form rendering can be obtained from the RAP Business Services.
  • The data model can be defined just as well as the RAP Service Definition.
  • The CL_FP_FDP_SERVICES class provides the ABAP API to receive the data in xml format for the use in print forms.
  • XML Schema Definition (XSD) used for the design of print forms in the Adobe LiveCycle Designer.

Initiate Business Data Reader

To supply the rendering operation with business data, a reuse service can be applied.

Service definition needs to be created previously.

The service definition doesn't need a binding for RAP Data Services for print forms.

Initiate the reuse utility by specifying the service definition.

DATA(lo_fdp_api) = cl_fp_fdp_services=>get_instance( ‘FP_FDP_SERVICE_EX1’ ).

Specify the key parameters.

This is required to read the data for a specific item.

The reuse utility offers the functionality to retrieve all key fields for the service definition.

DATA(lt_keys)    = lo_fdp_api->get_keys( ).

Once the array with key values has been retrieved, the keys that fit are assigned to the document to be output.

lt_keys[ name = 'UUID' ]-value = '4500000001'.

Retrieve XML Data from Service Definition

The READ_TO_XML_V2 method retrieves the data structure of the service definition for a specific item.

It has the Importing parameters IT_SELECT - Key parameters, IV_LANGUAGE (Optional).

It has the returning parameter RV_XML - Resulting XML as XSTRING.

TRY.
    DATA(lv_data) = lo_fdp_api->read_to_xml_v2( lt_keys ).
CATCH cx_fp_fdp_error INTO DATA(lo_exception).
ENDTRY.

Retrieve XML Schema Definition from Service Definition

The GET_XSD_V2 method retrieves the XML Schema Definition (XSD) for the service definition.

It has the returning parameter RV_XML, it returns XSD as XSTRING.

TRY.
   DATA(lv_xml)     = lo_fdp_api->get_xsd_v2(  ).
CATCH cx_fp_fdp_error INTO DATA(lo_exception).
ENDTRY

Runtime API for ADS Rendering Calls

The CL_FP_ADS_UTIL class provides the ABAP Runtime API for Adobe Document Services (ADS) rendering calls.

It contains the public method RENDER_PDF to be used for the Rendering PDF.

The PDF rendering process on ADS creates the PDF file to be printed (or print output format) using both formats

  • XDP form template file
  • Data for the form filling in XML format, for example, data from the RAP Data Services for Print Forms.

The XDP form template file is a description of the form layout design in Adobe XFA format.

The supported data binding type is an XML data schema.

The data XML file contains the data that corresponds to the used data schema in the XDP form template.

Rendering PDF

The RENDER_PDF method calls the ADS to render print PDF using the XDP form file and the XML data file. 

TRY.

*Render PDF
    cl_fp_ads_util=>render_pdf(
                             EXPORTING
                                   iv_xml_data      = lv_xml_data
                                   iv_xdp_layout    = lv_xdp
                                   iv_locale        = 'de_DE'
                                   is_options       = ls_options
                             IMPORTING
                                   ev_pdf           = ev_pdf
                                   ev_pages         = ev_pages
                                  ev_trace_string  = ev_trace_string ).
  CATCH cx_fp_ads_util INTO lx_fp_ads_util.
ENDTRY.

Importing Parameters

IV_XML_DATA - XML data

IV_XDP_LAYOUT - Adobe XDP form template

IV_LOCALE - Locale for rendering the language language_COUNTRY, (en_US)

IS_OPTIONS - PDF rendering parameters (optional)

Exporting Parameters

EV_PDF - PDF rendering result

EV_PAGES - Pages

EV_TRACE_STRING - Trace string

Creating Data Provider

Create Data Model

Create a data model, add the annotation to the CDS View to act a data provider for FORM Object.

      @ObjectModel.supportedCapabilities: [ #OUTPUT_FORM_DATA_PROVIDER ]

            supportedCapabilities defines which features or capabilities are supported by the CDS view or entity.

           #OUTPUT_FORM_DATA_PROVIDER is a specific capability flag that indicates the CDS entity can act as a data provider for output forms.

CDS View – ZI_FORM_PO_HD

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Form PO'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.supportedCapabilities: [ #OUTPUT_FORM_DATA_PROVIDER ]
define root view entity ZI_FORM_PO_HD
  as select from ZI_PO_HD_DT
{
  key PoNum,
      Supplier,
      PurOrg,
      CompCod,
      PurGrp,
      CrDt,
      _its
}

Data Source CDS View – ZI_PO_HD_DT

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'PO Header'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define root view entity ZI_PO_HD_DT
  as select from ztb_po_hdr
  association [0..*] to ZI_PO_ITS as _its on $projection.PoNum = _its.PoNum
{
  key po_num   as PoNum,
      supplier as Supplier,
      pur_org  as PurOrg,
      comp_cod as CompCod,
      pur_grp  as PurGrp,
      cr_dt    as CrDt,
      _its
}

Custom Table

@EndUserText.label : 'PO Header'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #ALLOWED
define table ztb_po_hdr {

  key client : abap.clnt not null;
  key po_num : abap.char(10) not null;
  supplier   : abap.char(10);
  pur_org    : abap.char(5);
  comp_cod   : abap.char(5);
  pur_grp    : abap.char(5);
  cr_dt      : abap.dats;

}

Create Service Definition

For a service definition, a leading entity can be defined using annotation

       @ObjectModel.leadingEntity.name: ''

  Add the CDS View as a leading entity.

@EndUserText.label: 'Service Definition of PO Form'
@ObjectModel.leadingEntity.name: 'ZI_FORM_PO_HD'
define service ZSR_FORM_PO_HD {
  expose ZI_FORM_PO_HD;
}

Service Definition ZSR_FORM_PO_HD is a Data provider for Form Object.

Service Binding

The service definition doesn't need a binding for RAP Data Services for print forms.

Creating Form Object

Create a Form Object with creation wizard from the main menu, toolbar, Project Explorer, or using a shortcut.

Enter the name and description of a form.

Select Next, assign a transport request, and select finish.

A new form is created and opened in the Form Editor.

A Data Provider now needs to be specified, which is defined in the system as a service containing the business data for this form.

In the General Information section, define the properties of the form

  • In the Data provider field, specify the service definition that should be used for fetching the business data for a form.
  • The Embed Font field should be enabled if the fonts are to be embedded into the generated PDF.
  • The Reduce Data Volume field should be enabled if the generated business data is to be filtered to only contain fields bound by fields in the form layout.

Choose Browse option to view the list of Data Providers.

Added the Data Provider ZSR_FORM_PO_HD to the Form

In the action menu, three options

  • Download Schema: Download the schema of the linked Data provider as an xsd file which can be imported into Adobe LiveCycle Designer for SAP solutions.
  • Upload Layout: Upload a layout file (*.xdp) created by Adobe LiveCycle Designer for SAP solutions (make sure the form should save before uploading a layout file).
  • Download Layout: Download the current layout uploaded to the form.

Choose Download Schema to create a new layout for the form.

Export and save schema to local files.

Open the Adobe Life Cycle Designer

Create a new blank form

Blank Form is created

Choose File -> New Data Connection

Choose XML Schema

Import the XML Schema File

Data Connection is added.

Design the layout

Save the layout file (XDP file) to local files.

Upload the Layout to the Form Object

Layout is uploaded, save the form object

Activate the Form Object

Layout can be downloaded with Download Layout option

Consuming the Form using Custom Entity

Custom Entity

Create a custom entity, add the form related fields and add the annotation to make its content as attachment.

@Semantics.largeObject:{ mimeType: 'MimyType', fileName: 'FileName',         
                         contentDispositionPreference: #ATTACHMENT }

Add the ABAP Class to implement the form access.

@EndUserText.label: 'PO Form'
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
@ObjectModel.query.implementedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_FORM_PO'
define custom entity ZI_FORM_PO

{
  key PO       : ebeln;
      @Semantics.largeObject:{ mimeType: 'MimyType', fileName: 'FileName', contentDispositionPreference: #ATTACHMENT }
      Form     : abap.rawstring( 0 );
      FileName : abap.char( 30 );
      MimyType : abap.char( 50 );
}

Class- ZCL_FORM_PO

CLASS zcl_form_po DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    INTERFACES if_rap_query_provider. 
PROTECTED SECTION.
PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS

CLASS zcl_form_po IMPLEMENTATION.

 METHOD if_rep_query_provider~select
     
      DATA: 1s_form TYPE zi_form_po,
            it_form TYPE TABLE OF zi_form_po.

      TRY.

           DATA(it_filter_conds) = io_request->get_filter()->get_as_ranges(). 
           LOOP AT it_filter_conds INTO DATA(1s_filter_conds).
            IF ls_filter_conds-name EQ 'PO'.
              READ TABLE ls_filter_conds-range INTO DATA(ls_rng) INDEX 1.
              IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
               DATA(lv_po) = ls_rng-low.
              ENDIF.
            ENDIF
            CLEAR: ls_rng.
           ENDLOOP.

         DATA(lv_paging) = io_request->get_paging( )->get_offset( ).
         DATA(lv_filters) = io_request->get_filter( )->get_as_sql_string( ).

       CATCH cx_rap_query_filter_no_range INTO DATA(cx_mg).

       IF 1v_po 15 INITIAL.
        lv_po = '4500000001'.
       ENDIF.

      TRY.
       "Initiate the Fore Template Reader
        DATA(lo_reader) = cl_fp_form_reader->create_form_reader( iv_formname = 'ZFORM_PO' ).
       "Read Layout Content to be Used by ADS Rendering Call 
        DATA(lv_layout) = lo_reader-get_layout().
      CATCH cx_fp_form_reader INTO DATA(ls_cx_fp_form_reader). 
      ENDTRY.

      TRY.
      "Get instance of Form
      DATA(lo_fdp_api) = cl_fdp_services->get instance(
                                  iv service definition = 'ZSR_FORM_PO_HD'
                                "  iv root node =
                                  iv_max_depth = 1 ).

      "Retrieve x Data from Service Definition
       DATA(ls_keys) = lo_fdp_api->get_keys( ).
       it_keys[ name = 'PONUM' ]->value = lv_po.
       
       DATA(lv_data) = lo_fdp_api->read_to_xml_v2( 
                                   it select = it_keys
                                  "it_select_add = 
                                  "iv_language = SY-LANGU 
                                     ).

       "Retrive XML Schema Definition from Definition
        DATA(lv_xml) = lo_fdp_api->get_xsd_v2( ).

      CATCH cx_fp_fdp_error INTO DATA(lo_exception).
     ENDTRY.

    TRY.
   "Rendering PDF
    cl_fp_ads_util=>render_pdf(
        EXPORTING
          iv_xml_data = lv_data   
          iv_xdp_layout = lv_layout
          iv_locale  = 'en_US'
       "   i_options = 
         IMPORTING
           ev_pdf = DATA(ev_pdf)
         ev_pages = 
         ev_trace_string =  
        ).
   
 CATCH cx_fp_ads_util INTO DATA(lo_fp_ads_util).

ENDTRY.

ls_form-po = lv_po.
ls_form-filename = 'Form_PO.pdf'.
ls_form-mimytype = 'application/pdf'.
ls_form-form = ev_pdf.
APPEND ls_form TO it_form.

io_response->set_data( it_form ).
io_response->set_total_number_of_records( lines( it_form ) ).

ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Service Definition

Create a Service Definition, expose the entity.

@EndUserText.label: 'FORM_PO'
define service ZSD_FORM_PO {
  expose ZI_FORM_PO;
}

Service Binding – Web API

Create a Service Binding with type OData V4 – Web API

Click on Service URL to access it.

Service URL

Access form details

Download Form with URL

Download the Form with accessing the service URL by passing document number.

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/zsrb_form_po/srvd_a2x/sap/zsd_form_po/0001/ZI_FORM_PO('4500000001')/Form

Form is downloaded

Service Binding – UI

Create a Service Binding with type OData V4 – UI

Download Form with UI

Choose Preview in service binding to view UI

Click on Form to download the Form

Form is downloaded

Debugging the Form Logic

Toggle Breakpoint is set

Click on Form to trigger the breakpoint.

Debugger got triggered.

Variables with values

Using CDS Views

CDS View

Create a CDS View with field having URL of Form

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'PO Header'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
define root view entity ZI_PO_HD
  as select from ztb_po_hdr

  association [0..*] to ZI_PO_ITS as _its on $projection.PoNum = _its.PoNum
{
  key po_num                                                                                                               as PoNum,
      supplier                                                                                                             as Supplier,
      pur_org                                                                                                              as PurOrg,
      comp_cod                                                                                                             as CompCod,
      pur_grp                                                                                                              as PurGrp,
      cr_dt                                                                                                                as CrDt,

      //      cast( 'PO Form.pdf' as abap.char( 20 ))                                                                              as FileName,
      //
      //      cast( 'application/pdf' as abap.char( 30 ))                                                                          as MimeType,

      concat( '/sap/opu/odata4/sap/zsb_form_po/srvd_a2x/sap/zsd_form_po/0001/ZI_FORM_PO(''', concat( po_num, ''')/Form' )) as FormURL,

      'Print PDF'                                                                                                          as Form,

      _its


}

Metadata Extension
